# barndoor_dlp_custom_detection_type (Resource)

Manages an organization-defined Data Protection (DLP) custom detection type: a named set of literal or regular-expression patterns that detection engines match alongside the built-in detection types.

The platform assigns the type's wire name (`detection_type`, of the form `DETECTION_TYPE_CUSTOM_…`) and places it in the `DETECTION_GROUP_CUSTOM` group; reference the generated `detection_type` from `barndoor_dlp_allow_list_entry.detection_types` or activity filters.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Detect internal project codenames. Patterns are evaluated in order; the
# platform assigns the type's wire name (DETECTION_TYPE_CUSTOM_…), exposed as
# `detection_type` for use in allow-list scoping and activity filters.
resource "barndoor_dlp_custom_detection_type" "codenames" {
  name        = "Project codenames"
  description = "Internal project codenames that must not leave the organization"

  patterns = [
    { pattern = "(?i)project\\s+aurora", pattern_type = "PATTERN_TYPE_REGEX" },
    { pattern = "AURORA-CLASSIFIED", pattern_type = "PATTERN_TYPE_LITERAL" },
  ]

  # Both default to *_MEDIUM when omitted.
  default_severity   = "DETECTION_SEVERITY_HIGH"
  default_confidence = "DETECTION_CONFIDENCE_HIGH"
}

# The generated wire name plugs into other Data Protection resources.
resource "barndoor_dlp_allow_list_entry" "codename_docs_site" {
  pattern         = "aurora.docs.example.com"
  pattern_type    = "PATTERN_TYPE_LITERAL"
  detection_types = [barndoor_dlp_custom_detection_type.codenames.detection_type]
  reason          = "Public documentation host, not a leak"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Human-readable display name of the detection type.
- `patterns` (Attributes List) Patterns that constitute the detection type, evaluated in order. At least one is required. Regular expressions use Rust `regex` syntax and are validated by the API (a non-compiling pattern is rejected with a 422). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--patterns))

### Optional

- `default_confidence` (String) Confidence findings of this type carry: `DETECTION_CONFIDENCE_LOW`, `DETECTION_CONFIDENCE_MEDIUM`, or `DETECTION_CONFIDENCE_HIGH`. Defaults to `DETECTION_CONFIDENCE_MEDIUM` when unset.
- `default_severity` (String) Severity findings of this type carry: `DETECTION_SEVERITY_LOW`, `DETECTION_SEVERITY_MEDIUM`, or `DETECTION_SEVERITY_HIGH`. Defaults to `DETECTION_SEVERITY_MEDIUM` when unset.
- `description` (String) What the detection type matches (free-form).

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (String) When the detection type was created (RFC 3339).
- `detection_group` (String) Detection group the type belongs to — always `DETECTION_GROUP_CUSTOM` for organization-defined types.
- `detection_type` (String) Generated wire name of the detection type (`DETECTION_TYPE_CUSTOM_<hex>`); this is the name findings carry and other resources (e.g. allow-list `detection_types`) reference.
- `id` (String) Detection type UUID assigned by the API; also the `terraform import` key.
- `org_id` (String) Organization the detection type belongs to, resolved from the provider credential's token claims.
- `updated_at` (String) When the detection type was last updated (RFC 3339).

<a id="nestedatt--patterns"></a>
### Nested Schema for `patterns`

Required:

- `pattern` (String) The literal value or regular expression to match, per `pattern_type`.
- `pattern_type` (String) How `pattern` matches: `PATTERN_TYPE_LITERAL` or `PATTERN_TYPE_REGEX`.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import by detection type UUID.
terraform import barndoor_dlp_custom_detection_type.codenames 11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111
```
